title: NG101 SCI Phase 2b: first MRI-imaging evidence of structural CNS protection
description: NervGen's NG101 Phase 2b spinal cord injury data now published in Nature Communications show faster lesion volume reduction and slower decline of spinal cord area and myelin-sensitive MRI measures vs. placebo. The first MRI-based human evidence of slowed neurodegeneration / fiber sprouting from a small molecule, with read-throughs to age-related CNS programs.

title: Eos SENOLYTIX / Pulmatrix merger closes 2026: GLP-1 alternative targeting mitochondria + senescent cells
description: The Pulmatrix-Eos reverse merger ($19M financing, Nasdaq listing under ticker SLYX) puts a first-in-class gerotherapeutic peptide platform (MitoXcel, lead PTC-2105) into a public vehicle. PTC-2105 simultaneously restores mitochondrial efficiency and induces apoptosis in senescent cells, positioning it as a differentiated GLP-1 adjunct that improves body composition without lean mass loss or rebound regain.

title: SIRT1 codified as the central exercise-mimetic mediator across tissues
description: New Biogerontology review pulls together cross-species evidence that exercise raises SIRT1 mRNA, activity, and protein in muscle, brain, heart, and adipose tissue. SIRT1 emerges as the most consistently exercise-responsive longevity gene — sharpening the therapeutic case for SIRT1-activating compounds and informing the geroscience case for movement protocols.

title: HuR/ELAVL1 in platelets identified as systemic aging accelerator
description: In Nature Communications, platelet-specific HuR/ELAVL1 deletion in aged mice reduced platelet-secreted pro-inflammatory factors, decreased organ infiltration by platelets, and delayed multiple systemic aging traits. Platelets emerge as an under-appreciated, druggable contributor to inflammaging — opening a new node beyond senescent cells and immune cells.

title: Physical activity meta-analysis (145,465 participants) confirms epigenetic age slowing
description: Across 44 studies and 145,465 participants, Lancet Healthy Longevity meta-analysis shows higher physical activity is associated with lower Horvath and GrimAge epigenetic age acceleration (though not Hannum/PhenoAge). The largest synthesis to date validates exercise as a population-scale geroprotector and supports its inclusion as a baseline layer in longevity-medicine protocols.

title: NG101 Enhances Regeneration in Human Spinal Cord Injury
description: Phase 2b SCI trial of NG101 shows faster lesion volume reduction and slower decline of spinal cord area and myelin-sensitive MRI measures vs. placebo — suggesting slowed degeneration or promoted fiber sprouting.

title: Sirtuin 1 Expression and Exercise-Induced Improvement in Metabolism
description: Review consolidates cross-species evidence that exercise raises SIRT1 mRNA, activity, or protein levels across muscle, brain, heart, and adipose tissues — proposing SIRT1 as a key mediator of healthy aging benefits.

title: HuR/ELAVL1 in Platelet-Driven Systemic Aging
description: Aged-mouse Nature Communications paper: platelet-specific HuR deletion reduced platelet-secreted pro-inflammatory factors, decreased organ infiltration, and delayed systemic aging traits.

title: Physical Activity and DNA Methylation Biological Age: Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
description: Across 44 studies and 145,465 participants, higher physical activity was associated with lower Horvath and GrimAge epigenetic age acceleration but not Hannum or PhenoAge — the largest meta-analysis to date.

title: Short-Term Dietary Intervention Alters Physiological Profiles Relevant to Ageing
description: 104 participants aged 65-75 saw a 4-week diet intervention change KDM-derived deltaAge; omnivorous/high-carbohydrate group showed a significant reduction vs. high-fat controls.

title: Aging, Intestinal Mucosal Immunity, and Gut Microbiota
description: In aged mice, intestinal barrier dysfunction, immune-cell imbalance, altered microbiota, and reduced M cell function were associated with inflammatory bacterial enrichment and dysregulated mucosal immunity.

title: TREM2 Signaling in Age-Related Macular Degeneration
description: Review discusses how retinal myeloid cells and TREM2 signaling may influence dry and neovascular AMD, and outlines translational challenges for targeting TREM2 therapeutically.

title: Cellular Senescence: From Pathogenic Mechanisms to Precision Anti-Aging Interventions
description: Review argues senescent cells are heterogeneous and that future senolytic strategies should focus on precision clearance of functionally harmful subpopulations rather than blanket removal.

title: Immune Priming with LPS Improves Wound Repair in Aged Skin
description: A short pre-injury LPS pulse accelerated wound closure in aged mouse skin by priming innate immune cells and improving epithelial/mesenchymal repair responses.

title: Role of Aging on Gamma Delta T Cells in Cutaneous Melanoma Progression
description: In melanoma studies across young, middle-aged, and geriatric mice, metastasis peaked in middle-aged animals — linked to age-dependent gamma delta T-cell changes and melanoma-derived PROS1.

title: Sociality and Longevity in Mammals (1,436 species)
description: Comparative analysis finds group-living and pair-living species have longer lifespans than solitary species after adjusting for body mass and phylogeny.

title: Pulmatrix and Eos SENOLYTIX Announce Merger Agreement
description: Pulmatrix (PULM) acquires Eos SENOLYTIX with $19M concurrent financing to advance lead clinical candidate PTC-2105 for sarcopenia and age-related disease via the MitoXcel mitochondrial-targeted geropeptide platform.

title: Methylene Blue May Help Protect Hair Follicle Stem Cells from GLP-1 Metabolic Stress
description: Maryland team shows methylene blue boosts HFSC proliferation via β-catenin activation, synergizes with minoxidil, and protects stem cells from GLP-1 receptor agonist-induced cell death.

title: Extending Healthspan Through Public Health and Longevity Medicine
description: New Aging-US editorial: healthy longevity will depend on coordinated integration of multiple intervention layers — public health, primary prevention, geroscience pharmaceuticals, and individualized longevity medicine.

title: Tozorakimab meets primary endpoint in Phase III MIRANDA COPD trial
description: AstraZeneca's IL-33 monoclonal antibody hit primary endpoint in MIRANDA Phase 3 COPD trial — validating IL-33 blockade as a disease-modifying mechanism for one of the most common diseases of aging.

title: U of Arizona launches $12M rapamycin Phase 3 trial in older adults
description: R. Ken Coit College of Pharmacy 2-year double-blind low-dose rapamycin trial in 65+ adults measuring frailty transitions and IL-6 inflammaging.

title: Insilico forms Longevity Board to oversee AI aging research
description: Insilico stands up dedicated Longevity Board (chaired by Lilly's Andrew Adams) to oversee its AI-driven aging research program, formalizing geroscience commitment alongside the $2.75B Lilly deal.

title: BioAge reports positive Phase 1 data for BGE-102
description: BioAge Labs reports positive Phase 1 results for BGE-102, its NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitor program, supporting further development in cardiometabolic and inflammaging indications.

title: Linnaeus Therapeutics secures up to $22M ARPA-H award for LNS8801
description: ARPA-H awards up to $22M to Linnaeus Therapeutics to advance LNS8801 for healthspan preservation, part of broader PROSPR cohort funding 7 academic / biotech teams targeting aging biology.

title: Immorta Bio advances senolytic immunotherapy + regenerative cell therapy toward IND
description: Immorta Bio releases preclinical lifespan-extension data combining senolytic immunotherapy with regenerative cell therapy; program advancing toward IND-enabling studies.

title: Human Longevity x LEV Foundation centenarian blood study
description: Human Longevity and LEV Foundation begin collaborative study of centenarian and supercentenarian blood to identify biological drivers of exceptionally healthy aging.

title: Seragon SRN-901 extends median lifespan in mice
description: Seragon Biosciences reports SRN-901 extended median remaining lifespan in 18-month-old mice with reduced frailty and tumor burden — a late-life rejuvenation signal.

title: AlzeCure completes Phase Ib study of ACD856
description: AlzeCure Pharma completes Phase Ib study of ACD856, a TrkA/TrkB positive modulator for Alzheimer's and cognitive disorders.

title: AB Science links masitinib to survival benefit in ALS trial
description: AB Science reports masitinib showed a survival benefit in an ALS trial, supporting tyrosine kinase modulation as an approach for neurodegeneration.

title: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging
description: NYT Magazine feature documents how rejuvenation has emerged as the most promising longevity advance, with Life Biosciences eye-disease trials providing first human safety read-out from Yamanaka factor partial reprogramming.
